hero.title: 7 Days, Triangle & Golden Temple.
hero.titleAccent: Delhi, Agra, Jaipur and the Golden Temple.
hero.intro: The classic Golden Triangle with a golden finish. Delhi, the Taj Mahal and Jaipur's forts, then up to Amritsar for the Golden Temple and the Wagah border. Seven days of India's greatest northern icons.
hero.heroImageAlt: Golden Triangle and Amritsar Golden Temple tour

overview.heading: 7 Days Golden Triangle + Amritsar: The Full Picture
body: This 7 day tour adds the spiritual highlight of Amritsar to the classic Golden Triangle. You experience Delhi's history, the Taj Mahal at sunrise and Jaipur's royal forts, then travel to Amritsar for the Golden Temple, the langar and the famous Wagah border ceremony.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip links four of North India's greatest destinations with your own vehicle and driver guide. It is an ideal itinerary for travellers who want the iconic monuments of the Golden Triangle plus the deep spiritual atmosphere of Sikhism's holiest shrine.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Taj Mahal & Jaipur's Forts
items.body: The Taj Mahal at sunrise, Agra Fort, and Jaipur's hilltop Amber Fort and Pink City palaces, the heart of the Golden Triangle.

items.title: The Golden Temple, Amritsar
items.body: Harmandir Sahib at dawn and after dark, the free langar, and the moving spiritual atmosphere of Sikhism's holiest shrine.

items.title: Old Delhi & the Wagah Border
items.body: The spice lanes and grand mosques of Old Delhi, and the high-energy flag-lowering ceremony at the Wagah border each evening.

days.title: Arrive Delhi
days.description: Met at Delhi airport and transferred to your hotel.

days.title: Delhi, Drive to Agra
days.description: Old and New Delhi, then drive to Agra.

days.title: Taj Mahal, Agra Fort, Drive to Jaipur
days.description: Sunrise Taj, Agra Fort, then to Jaipur via Fatehpur Sikri.

days.title: Jaipur Sightseeing
days.description: A full day in the Pink City.

days.title: Jaipur to Amritsar
days.description: Travel to Amritsar (flight or via Delhi), evening at the Golden Temple.

days.title: Amritsar & Wagah Border
days.description: Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh and the Wagah border.

days.title: Depart Amritsar
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Four Icons, One Seamless Trip
whyBook.body: Linking the Golden Triangle with Amritsar takes good routing and timing for the Taj and Wagah. We sequence it so the journey flows. Dhesu has run North India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Golden Triangle & Amritsar Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 7 day package combines the classic Golden Triangle of Delhi, Agra and Jaipur with the spiritual highlight of Amritsar and the Golden Temple.

A private vehicle and driver guide link the iconic monuments with Sikhism's holiest shrine, including the Taj at sunrise and the famous Wagah border ceremony.

It is an ideal itinerary for travellers who want both the monumental and the spiritual sides of North India in one trip.

sections.heading: Adding Amritsar to the Golden Triangle
sections.body:
Amritsar is a natural and rewarding addition to the Golden Triangle. The Golden Temple offers a profound spiritual experience quite different from the Mughal monuments and Rajput forts of the triangle.

The langar, the dawn and evening temple visits, and the theatrical Wagah border ceremony round out a trip that already has the Taj Mahal and Jaipur's palaces, giving a fuller picture of the north.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Delhi and Amritsar Airports (DEL / ATQ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: October to March is ideal across all four destinations, with comfortable days and cool nights. April to June is very hot on the plains. July to September is the monsoon. Winter offers the best conditions for the Taj at sunrise and the Wagah border.

items.question: How do I travel from Jaipur to Amritsar?
items.answer: Typically by a domestic flight or via Delhi, which we arrange as part of the package to keep the journey efficient. We will confirm the exact routing based on your dates and preferences.

items.question: Is the Wagah border ceremony included?
items.answer: Yes. The package includes a visit to the Wagah border for the famous evening flag-lowering ceremony, one of the highlights of any Amritsar visit. Your guide handles the timing for a good seat.
